query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research and provided both a full markdown synthesis and structured findings. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**The reported pause/drop:** The most significant pause was a **7-day complete absence of PLA aircraft** near Taiwan's ADIZ from **Feb 27 to ~Mar 5–7, 2026** — the longest recorded. Analysts (Taiwan Security Monitor/GMU) attributed it most plausibly to China's "Two Sessions" and Spring/Lantern Festival timing, concluding it was routine variability rather than a strategic shift. Naval assets stayed active throughout.

**Sustained lower trend in 2026:** Air activity has been broadly down across H1 2026 — ~460 aircraft in Jan–early March (a 46.5% drop vs. 2025), and June 2026 saw only 214 total sorties / 134 median-line crossings, both far below 2024–2025 levels. Meanwhile naval activity hit a record June high of 225 PLAN vessels.

**Typical recent daily counts:** Single digits to low-20s (e.g., May 28: 9; Jun 6: 22; Jul 1: 13; Jul 2: 22), averaging roughly ~7 aircraft/day in June.

**Highest single-day count:** The largest surge tied to 2026 was **90 warplanes crossing the median line on Dec 30–31, 2025** (New Year drills). Within the clean Jan–Jun 2026 window, single-day peaks were only ~24–29 aircraft — well below Taiwan's all-time record of 153 (Oct 2024).

One caveat: I could not directly extract the CSIS ChinaPower spreadsheet or a discrete May 2026 monthly total (only June's monthly aggregate was available); daily May figures were captured from MND-sourced news reporting.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From Feb 27 to Mar 5, 2026, Taiwan MND reported no PLA aircraft near the ADIZ — a 7-day pause likely tied to Two Sessions and festival periods.

sources.title: Chinese Warplanes Return to Taiwan's ADIZ After Weeklong Absence
sources.snippet: Seven-day absence Feb 27; Jan–early Mar 2026 saw 460 PLA aircraft, a 46.5% decrease vs prior year; naval/coast guard remained active.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang — Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: June 2026: 134 median line crossings, 214 total sorties (both far below 2024/2025); 225 PLAN vessels — highest June on record.

sources.title: 90 PLA warplanes cross Taiwan Strait median line as China stages drills
sources.snippet: Ninety PLA warplanes crossed the median line in 24h Dec 30–31, 2025 during military exercises.

sources.title: PLA sends 8 vessels, 24 aircraft across median line
sources.snippet: 24 military aircraft crossed the median line between Mar 17-18, 2026, entering northern, central, southwestern and eastern ADIZ.

sources.title: PLA military flights off Taiwan resume
sources.snippet: Five PLA aircraft detected Mar 11-12, 2026 following the mysterious pause; three crossed the median line.

sources.title: Taiwan tracks 22 military aircraft, 10 Chinese ships (Jun 6, 2026)
sources.snippet: 22 PLA aircraft, 8 naval vessels, 2 official ships between Jun 5-6, 2026; 2 aircraft entered central/southwestern ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 9 PLA aircraft sorties, 6 PLAN vessels (May 28, 2026)
sources.snippet: 9 sorties of PLA aircraft, all crossing the median line, detected up to 6 a.m. May 28, 2026.

sources.title: Taiwan detects rise in Chinese military incursions (Jul 1, 2026)
sources.snippet: 13 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 naval vessels, 3 official ships as of 6 a.m. Jul 1, 2026; 9 crossed the median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 22 Chinese aircraft sorties (Jul 2, 2026)
sources.snippet: 22 sorties (incl. J-16, H-6, KJ-500) detected Jul 2, 2026; 20 crossed the median line.

sources.title: Taiwan ADIZ Violations dataset — ChinaPower Project (CSIS)
sources.snippet: Authoritative dataset by Gerald C. Brown & Ben Lewis on Chinese air incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ; all-time single-day record 153 aircraft (Oct 14, 2024).

sources.title: China's Taiwan Drills Are Crossing a New Line
sources.snippet: Analysis of late-Dec 2025 Justice Mission 2025 large-scale drills disrupting civilian air routes near Taiwan.
